description: Registry of every web game built by gh-game-factory. Never duplicate an entry — check name, description, category, and genre before building.

games.name: gloamrun
games.description: Endless co-op dungeon crawler: descend procedurally generated floors against an ever-worsening swarm — you auto-fire at the nearest monster so the game is all in your feet (position, kite, dash with i-frames), clear the floor, step on the stair, draft one of three upgrades, go deeper. Downed players are revivable by a teammate, never eliminated; the run ends only on a party wipe, and clearing a floor revives the party (floors are checkpoints). Fully playable solo; live P2P co-op for 2-4 over a shared room code. Host-authoritative star (host runs monster sim + floor machine and broadcasts a full snapshot, clients send input and predict their own hero); host transfer promotes a survivor who keeps the run finishable. Three modes with real spread (Delve roomy, Onslaught crowds, Gauntlet tight elites), boss every few floors. The difficulty curve is the opponent: a mandatory sim (tests/balance.test.ts) refereed the ramp and caught a 4-player immortal-party bug (out-DPSing a polynomial ramp forever) — fixed with a geometric HP term past floor 5 that guarantees termination. Also back-ported the stale rematch `cur` catch-up fix into patterns/.

games.name: delvepack
games.description: Co-op top-down dungeon crawler: descend procedurally-generated floors, auto-fire at the nearest monster, dash to dodge (i-frames), clear a floor to drop upgrade orbs and light the exit rune, then stand on it to descend — the ramp is the whole difficulty. Solo score-attack (deepest floor) plus live P2P for 2-4 delvers in the SAME deterministic dungeon against the dungeon, reviving downed teammates over a bleed-out window (all-down = wipe; a dropped peer just makes the run harder). Host-authoritative snapshot star: host owns monsters/bolts/HP/floor/rune, each peer owns only its own delver's motion; host transfer promotes a survivor who rebuilds combat stats from the deterministic formula and keeps the run finishable. Three modes that change the SPACE not a dial (Delve mid-hall, Warren wide crowd-cavern, Crypt tight knife-fight). NOTE: built concurrently with (and independent of) ben-gy/gloamrun from the same IDEAS.md line — a shared-queue race; both shipped. The co-op difficulty curve is refereed by an AI-vs-the-dungeon balance sim that OVERRULED the design (crowds+revive-cascades made co-op a tax, not deeper) and surfaced two real softlocks (a monster unkillable behind a pillar; an orb-fixation stall), both fixed and mutation-guarded.

games.name: morsel
games.description: Petri-dish free-for-all: eat pellets to grow, swallow any smaller blob whole, run from bigger ones. Big blobs are slow, so the only way to catch anyone is to DASH — and a dash costs 15% of your mass, sprayed out behind you as food for whoever you're chasing, so the only way to grow your lead is to spend it. Pellets ramp in value over the round (the bloom) so an early lead banks nothing and the last stretch decides it; being swallowed just costs 1.5s and nobody is ever out. Three modes (Skirmish tiny/6/110s, Petri medium/6/150s, Famine big/8/180s with a third of the food); solo vs bots, live P2P free-for-all for 2-8, or an async seed-share. Split authority: each peer owns its own blob's motion and death, the host owns the pellet field, bots, clock and mass ledger; host transfer promotes a survivor who keeps the round finishable. Balance simulated AI-vs-AI (P(leader wins) flat early then spikes, every seat within a few points of chance) — the design doc predicted a snowball; the sim proved the opposite and drove the fix.

games.name: driftlock
games.description: Abstract board game on a wrapping grid where you never touch a stone — you shift the whole row or column it stands on, everything slides and wraps, and any stone that lands in the central Well banks for whoever made the shift (stones are neutral, so there is no seat geometry to make unfair). The line you just shifted is locked for your opponent's next turn, so the game is about leaving them nothing to take. The Well rises as a pure function of the move number and stones are worth more the later they land, so an early lead is not a won game — a design the balance sim refereed and overruled repeatedly (documented in src/game.ts). Solo vs 3-strength bots, live P2P for 2, and async seed-share; three modes (Blitz 5-stone, Duel 9-stone, Deepwell 9x9).

games.name: windup
games.description: Simultaneous-programming strategy: slot three movement cards into your wind-up bot, then every bot's program executes at once, one step at a time — and bots shove each other, so where you end up is rarely where you planned. Cogs ripen (+1 value a round, up to 5) so the board starts cheap and ends enormous, and everyone converges on the fat centre cog at the climax. Falling in a pit costs tempo, never elimination. Three modes (Skirmish 9x9/8 rounds, Clockwork 11x11/12, Gauntlet 13x13/16 with four program slots); solo vs bots, live P2P for 2-4, or an async seed-share challenge. Board is 90-degree rotationally symmetric with cogs spawning in symmetric orbits, so turn-0 is provably fair.
games.status_note: Deploy succeeded and every production asset serves 200 (HTML, JS, CSS, manifest, all four icons), with the [hidden] guard confirmed in the shipped CSS. Full in-browser play + two-peer P2P smoke test (typed-code join, in-sync play, host stickiness, host-leave -> survivor finishes) all passed against the byte-identical local build of this commit. NOT marked deployed because the browser tooling (Chrome extension + in-app pane) went down before a visual pass against the live URL and a ~375px mobile screenshot could be taken.

games.name: bidstorm
games.description: Bidding card game with no luck in the deal: every player holds the identical hand 1-13 and secretly commits one card per flipped prize, highest bid takes the pot and every card played is burned forever, while a tie for the lead wins nothing and carries the pot into the next round. Every hand is public, so it's a decision rather than a guess. Three modes (Classic, Foresight's two-prize lookahead, Blitz's seven cards where a tie burns the prize); solo vs bots, live P2P for 2-6, or an async seed-share challenge.

games.name: lumenlock
games.description: Light-routing puzzle: a white beam hits a prism and fans into a rainbow (red bends left, green straight, blue right); flip mirrors and half-silvered splitters to lock every crystal in its exact colour, where a crystal sums everything it receives so a stray beam spoils it and a white crystal takes either untouched white light or red+green+blue recombined. Solo, with an exhaustively-searched true par, a UTC Daily Lock and seeded share links.

games.name: nightwire
games.description: Hidden-role social deduction on a ring of seats: each night you probe a seat and learn how many Ghosts sit in that 3-seat window, but while Crew readings publish automatically and truthfully, Ghosts choose what number to publish — eject every Ghost before the nightly wire cuts cause a blackout; solo vs solver bots or live P2P for 4-10 humans.

games.name: orbital-skirmish
games.description: Momentum dogfight in a circular arena with a lethal star at the centre that pulls ships, bullets AND rocks — so you never aim where a rival is, you aim where gravity will carry the shot, and a bullet inherits your velocity so charging is brutal and fleeing is feeble. Solo Survival (escalating AI waves, five lives, instant play) plus live P2P deathmatch for 2–4, host-authoritative snapshot star, rematch inside the room with a running tally. Three modes that change the spatial problem not a dial: Skirmish (open arena), Nova (the star flares on a nine-second clock), Belt (weak star, dense rock). Spawns sit on a rotationally symmetric ring so no seat starts ahead — a fairness the mandatory balance sim measured rather than assumed (it found two real bugs and refuted two confident diagnoses; see src/modes.ts and tests/balance.test.ts). Finishes the 2026-07-13 slot the original run abandoned after 70 seconds.

games.name: gravity-golf
games.description: Slingshot physics mini-golf: pull back to launch and bend your ball around gravity wells (attractors pull, repulsors push, black holes swallow) into the hole across 9 procedural holes; solo, async seed-share, or live P2P race up to 6 players.

games.name: snake-royale
games.description: Classic snake in a shared arena: eat pellets to grow and score, avoid walls/snakes/your own tail; solo Endless score-attack plus live P2P Royale where the last snake alive wins.

games.name: rhythm-relay
games.description: Two-lane rhythm game: notes relay left↔right toward a hit line; tap in time to build a shared combo and score multiplier, misses drain energy; solo or take a lane each in live P2P co-op.

games.name: hexbloom
games.description: Turn-based hex-grid area control: pick a colour to recolour your territory and absorb every touching tile of it; largest territory when the board fills wins.

games.name: cipher-clash
games.description: Boggle-style word-grid race; drag touching letters to spell words, and in versus a word only scores for whoever claims it first.
